title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
seo.metaDescription: Warm up with our authentic Mercimek Corbasi, a traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This easy recipe brings the flavors of Turkey to your kitchen. Perfect for a cozy meal!

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, comforting flavors of Mercimek Corbasi, a beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup perfect for any occasion.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its rich flavor and comforting warmth. This hearty dish is a staple in Turkish homes, often served as a starter or a light meal. Its vibrant color and smooth texture make it an inviting and nutritious option for any time of year, especially with a squeeze of fresh lemon.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Vegetables
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear; set aside. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot and saute until softened, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Build the Flavor Base
instructions.text: Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the tomato paste and flour, cooking for 2-3 minutes while stirring constantly to form a roux and cook out the raw flour taste.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable or chicken broth and immediately whisk well to prevent lumps. Add the rinsed red lentils, salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup
instructions.text: Once the lentils are cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender to blend the soup directly in the pot until it is smooth and creamy. If you dont have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chili Mint Butter
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and slightly foamy, add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (pul biber). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the warm chili mint butter over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, which adds a wonderful brightness to the soup.

tips: For an extra smooth soup, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve after blending, though it's usually not necessary with an immersion blender.
The chili mint butter (nane-pul biberli yağ) is crucial for authentic flavor. Adjust the amount of red pepper flakes to your spice preference.
Always serve Mercimek Corbasi with fresh lemon wedges. The acidity of the lemon brightens the flavors and is a traditional accompaniment.
article:
Mercimek Corbasi: The Soul-Warming Embrace of Turkish Red Lentil Soup

In the vast and delectable world of Turkish cuisine, few dishes evoke as much warmth, comfort, and universal appeal as Mercimek Corbasi. This vibrant, creamy, and incredibly flavorful red lentil soup is a staple in Turkish homes, a beloved starter in restaurants, and a testament to the country's rich culinary heritage. Whether you're seeking a nutritious meal, a comforting bowl on a chilly day, or an authentic taste of Turkey, learning to make this iconic soup is a culinary journey well worth taking.

## What is Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced mer-jee-MEK chor-BAH-suh, translates directly to "lentil soup." However, in Turkey, when one simply says "mercimek corbasi," it almost exclusively refers to the red lentil version. It's a cornerstone of Turkish soups and stews, celebrated for its simplicity, nutritional value, and deeply satisfying taste. Unlike some lentil soups that might be chunky or rustic, Mercimek Corbasi is typically smooth and velvety, achieved through blending, giving it a luxurious mouthfeel.

Its characteristic bright orange-red hue comes from the red lentils themselves, often enhanced by a touch of tomato paste and a generous drizzle of a spiced butter or oil topping (known as *nane ve pul biberli tereyağı* or simply *sos*). This topping, made with melted butter or olive oil infused with dried mint and Aleppo pepper (pul biber), is crucial. It adds an aromatic flourish, a hint of spice, and a beautiful visual contrast, elevating the soup from simple to sublime. Beyond its deliciousness, Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly healthy, packed with protein, fiber, and essential nutrients, making it a perfect meal for any time of day. It's truly a dish that nourishes both body and soul.

## Ingredients You Need for Mercimek Corbasi

One of the beauties of this Turkish Red Lentil Soup is its reliance on simple, readily available ingredients. You don't need exotic spices or hard-to-find vegetables to create an authentic and delicious bowl.

*   **Red Lentils (Kırmızı Mercimek):** The star of the show! Use split red lentils, as they cook quickly and break down easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture. About 1.5 cups is a good starting point.
*   **Onion:** One medium-sized onion, finely chopped, forms the aromatic base.
*   **Garlic:** 2-3 cloves, minced, for depth of flavor.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** 1-2 tablespoons. This adds umami, richness, and contributes to the soup's beautiful color.
*   **Vegetable or Chicken Broth/Stock:** 6-8 cups. While water works, a good quality broth will significantly enhance the flavor.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** For sautéing the aromatics and for the topping. About 2-3 tablespoons for cooking, plus more for the topping.
*   **Dried Mint (Kuru Nane):** 1-2 teaspoons for the soup itself, and another teaspoon for the topping. This is a non-negotiable flavor for authentic Mercimek Corbasi.
*   **Aleppo Pepper (Pul Biber) or Paprika:** 1-2 teaspoons, for the topping. Pul biber offers a mild, fruity heat; if unavailable, sweet paprika with a pinch of cayenne can be a substitute.
*   **Salt and Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Optional for Serving:** Fresh lemon wedges, crusty bread, a dollop of plain yogurt.

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Making a truly authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup is a straightforward process that yields incredibly rewarding results. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl every time:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any starch and ensures a cleaner flavor. You don't need to soak red lentils as they cook very quickly.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent, being careful not to brown them. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Spices:** Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add 1 teaspoon of dried mint and stir briefly.
4.  **Combine with Lentils and Broth:** Add the rinsed lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the aromatics. Pour in the vegetable or chicken broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend the Soup:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it's completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (be extremely careful with hot liquids, filling the blender only halfway and holding the lid securely with a towel). Blend until smooth, then return to the pot.
6.  **Season and Adjust Consistency:** Return the pot to low heat. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot broth or water until it reaches your desired consistency. Let it simmer for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
7.  **Prepare the Mint-Chili Topping:** While the soup simmers, melt 2-3 tablespoons of butter (or olive oil) in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once melted, remove from heat and stir in 1 teaspoon of dried mint and 1-2 teaspoons of Aleppo pepper (or paprika/cayenne blend). The heat from the butter will bloom the spices, releasing their aroma. Be careful not to burn the spices.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a generous amount of the spiced butter topping over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges and crusty bread.

## Pro Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

Achieving that perfect, authentic taste and texture for your Turkish Red Lentil Soup is all about a few key details:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils!** Don't skip rinsing. It removes excess starch that can make the soup cloudy or gummy.
*   **Bloom the Tomato Paste:** Cooking the tomato paste for a minute or two before adding liquids enhances its flavor and reduces its raw acidity, contributing to a richer soup base.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Broth:** While water works, a good quality vegetable or chicken broth will add incredible depth of flavor. If using water, consider adding a bouillon cube or powder.
*   **Blend Until Velvety Smooth:** The signature texture of Mercimek Corbasi is its creaminess. Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to ensure no lumps remain.
*   **The Power of Dried Mint:** Dried mint is absolutely essential for authentic flavor. Fresh mint won't give you the same result in this soup.
*   **The Topping is Non-Negotiable:** That vibrant, spiced butter or oil drizzle isn't just for looks; it adds an aromatic burst of flavor and a crucial layer of authenticity. Don't skip it!
*   **Season Generously:** Lentils can absorb a lot of seasoning. Taste and adjust salt and pepper multiple times throughout the cooking process and before serving. A squeeze of lemon at the end also brightens all the flavors.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Turkish Lentil Soup

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and pairs well with a variety of accompaniments, enhancing its appeal as a comforting and satisfying meal.

*   **Lemon Wedges:** This is a non-negotiable. A generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before eating brightens the soup, balances its richness, and elevates all the flavors.
*   **Crusty Bread:** Essential for scooping up every last drop! Fresh Turkish pide, sourdough, or even a simple baguette works wonderfully.
*   **Plain Yogurt:** A dollop of cool, plain yogurt (or a drizzle of homemade yogurt sauce with a touch of garlic) can add a lovely creamy tang, contrasting beautifully with the warm, spiced soup.
*   **Simple Salad:** A light, fresh salad like Shepherd's Salad (Çoban Salata) with tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and parsley dressed with olive oil and lemon juice, provides a refreshing counterpoint.
*   **Pickled Vegetables (Turşu):** Many Turks enjoy their soup with a side of crunchy pickled vegetables, adding a delightful sour kick.
*   **Extra Chili Flakes:** For those who love more heat, offer extra pul biber or red pepper flakes on the side.

## Variations and Substitutions for Red Lentil Soup

While the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup recipe is perfect as is, there are several ways you can adapt it to your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Vegan Version:** Simply ensure you use vegetable broth instead of chicken broth, and opt for olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and the topping. The rest of the ingredients are naturally vegan.
*   **Adding Vegetables:** For extra nutrition and texture, you can add finely diced carrots or potatoes along with the lentils. They will cook down and blend seamlessly into the soup. A diced bell pepper can also be sautéed with the onions.
*   **Spicier Kick:** If you enjoy more heat, increase the amount of Aleppo pepper in the topping, or add a pinch of cayenne pepper to the soup while it simmers.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A tiny pinch of smoked paprika can add an interesting depth to the soup, though it's not traditional.
*   **Different Herbs:** While dried mint is king, a pinch of dried oregano can also be a nice addition to the soup's base.
*   **Creamier Texture:** For an even richer, creamier soup, you can stir in a splash of milk or cream (dairy or non-dairy) at the very end, just before serving, though this isn't typical for the classic version.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a culinary hug in a bowl. Its simplicity, combined with its profound depth of flavor and comforting warmth, makes it an enduring favorite. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a kitchen novice, attempting this Turkish Red Lentil Soup will not only reward you with a delicious meal but also connect you to the heart of Turkish home cooking. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to fall in love with this unforgettable dish.
